entry.title: Transforming MRO Planning with SAP Integrated Business Planning
entry.content:
In industries where asset uptime is critical, Maintenance, Repair, and Operations (MRO) processes often fall into a reactive mode, struggling with inefficiencies and unexpected downtime. SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) offers a transformative approach that aligns maintenance needs with supply chain capabilities, driving efficiencies and ensuring asset availability.

The Challenges in MRO Management
Maintenance operations face the perpetual challenge of balancing between minimizing inventory costs and ensuring the availability of spare parts and resources to prevent asset downtime. Traditional MRO approaches are characterized by:

- Reactive Maintenance Strategies:  Often leading to higher costs and operational disruptions.
- Disjointed Planning: Maintenance and supply chain teams working in silos, resulting in misaligned objectives and inefficiencies.
- Excess or Insufficient Inventory: Causing high carrying costs or critical stock-outs.
These challenges highlight the necessity for a more integrated, proactive planning approach that SAP IBP facilitates.

Key Modules in SAP IBP Tailored for MRO
Here’s how the key modules within SAP IBP can specifically enhance MRO activities:

1. Demand Forecasting
- Purpose: Provides accurate predictions of spare parts demand, factoring in historical usage, predictive maintenance data, and machine learning insights.
- Outcome: Ensures spare parts are available when needed, preventing downtime and excess inventory.

2. Inventory Optimization
- Purpose: Sets optimal inventory levels to meet targeted service levels while minimizing holding costs.
- Outcome: Balances inventory costs with availability, ensuring critical parts are stocked without overburdening storage facilities.

3. Supply Planning
- Purpose: Determine exception management with alerts for parts and resources using Infinite Heuristics.
- Outcome: Parts Plan and Resource Plan 

Integrating Maintenance with Supply Chain Execution
SAP IBP achieves integration by aligning maintenance activities directly with supply chain operations through real-time data and analytics:
Integration with SAP S/4HANA: SAP IBP connects seamlessly, offering a unified approach that aligns enterprise resource planning with maintenance scheduling.
- Data-Driven Insights: Utilizes operational data to drive planning processes, enabling a responsive and adaptable strategy.

Benefits of SAP IBP for MRO
The deployment of SAP IBP in MRO processes offers several compelling benefits:
- Enhanced Asset Utilization: Proactively managing maintenance strategies improves asset availability and performance.
- Cost Savings: Reducing inventory and optimizing resource allocation leads to significant cost reductions.
- Improved Collaboration: Cross-functional collaboration aligns maintenance, supply chain, and financial planning.

Real-World Success: Microsoft’s MRO Optimization
Microsoft’s data center operations, SAP IBP have delivered:
- Inventory Reduction: Achieving millions in savings by optimizing spare parts inventory.
- Operational Efficiency: Streamlined supply chains and improved response to maintenance needs.

The Road Ahead: Continuous Innovation
SAP continues to enhance IBP’s capabilities with features such as predictive analytics and AI-driven insights, making it an invaluable tool for predictive maintenance and strategic resource planning.
SAP IBP for MRO provides a comprehensive solution to the complexities of maintenance planning and execution. By leveraging integration, advanced analytics, and collaborative planning processes, businesses can achieve a balanced, efficient, and proactive maintenance strategy tailored for today's dynamic environments

IBP for MRO assets:

  • IBP for MRO: microsite
  • Maintenance, Repair, and Operations process in IBP: Help page
  • Maintenance, Repair, and Operations Best practice: Best practice content
  • MRO Inventory Planning Operator in IBP: Help page

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Story Report Deep Link with Filter Cri...
entry.content:
Hello @RageshVr 

Passing input parameter values through a deeplink is not supported.

However, you can save a filter state and reuse it when accessing the story. Additionally, you can configure a default filter to be applied automatically when the story is opened.

For more details, please refer to the following documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/story-reports/bookmark-and-share-story-views

Thanks,

Raghu

I'm also confused about this anouncement and it is a real bummer for me as lots of my business colleagues wants to develop their own apps acting as "citizen developers".

Anyhow I understand that SAP Build Apps will be discontinued as a standalone subscription. But I still don't understand what we should provide to our internal customers as alternative? In SAP Discovery Center, SAP Build Apps is stated as part of SAP Build offer.

Ok lets try something else and start with a fresh BTP trial environment. Even here, there is no single SAP Build service I can subscribe but it still shows SAP Build Apps as individual service without any warning or deprecation sign.

What is the right service to choose? Which product offers low-code development my colleagues are asking for?

entry.title: Re: Automatically update the storage location at the sale...
entry.content:
Hi,

No, this is NOT possible in  SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to automatically update storage location during sales order creation/save based on ship-to party. In S/4HANA Public Cloud, storage location is not determined at sales order level in standard process. It is automatically determined during outbound delivery, based on configuration like: plant, shipping point etc.

Regards

entry.title: Re: email setting for billing form
entry.content:
Hello Priya,

What is not possible in Public Cloud

You cannot extend OPD using unreleased CDS views (like C_BILLINGDOCUMENTOPARAMDET)
You cannot add custom ABAP logic inside OPD
Classic output condition logic available in On‑Premise does not apply

So the solution must use the released extensibility only.

* SAP‑Supported Solution (Recommended)
* Use a Custom Field + OPD Condition
This is the only clean and SAP‑compliant approach today.


Thanks 
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Custom Workflow for Purchase Requisition Based on Hea...
entry.content:
Good day @Shujaat_Haider 

Thank you for your question.

If your custom fields are already available in the workflow conditions and are also visible on the header of the Purchase Requisition in Create Purchase Requisition-Advanced, then you can use those fields to trigger a custom workflow in Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions. The standard setup is to use the custom field as a start condition in the workflow definition.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/f83f5260160d40a4ac1797e7d8b1eb5e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/bacc21e14d6c4b0fbf53dca3218abdd5-323.html?locale=en-US

The approach I recommend is:

1. In Custom Fields, verify the custom field is created for the relevant Purchase Requisition business context. Then ensure the field is published and enabled for the UI and workflow related usage. You can extend Purchase Requisition through Custom Fields and its logic.

2. In Manage Workflow for Purchase Requisitions, create or adjust your workflow. In Start Conditions, select the custom field. Maintain the value that should trigger the workflow and define the approvers and activate the workflow.

3. Finally, you can carry out a test in Create Purchase Requisition-Advanced. Create a requisition, enter your required custom field value at header level, save and submit. Verify that your intended workflow is triggered.

4. If your field is visible on the requisition but does not appear as a selectable workflow condition, then you can use a custom pre-condition in Custom Fields as a fallback. See KBA 2767845 on the procurement pre-conditions.

So, in summary, the answer is yes, the workflow can be triggered based on your custom fields:

  • Use the custom field directly as a workflow start condition if it is available in the workflow app.
  • Otherwise use a custom pre-condition through Custom Fields.

 

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/720dcec59d264114bc4ec19759aa4514.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/003174573d144755b4899fad743c45aa.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SCMON Setup and Execution Guide
entry.content:
SCMON Setup and Execution Guide

Purpose: The ABAP Call Monitor (SCMON) is a tool designed to monitor the execution and usage of ABAP code (function modules, method calls, etc.) in your productive SAP system. It helps identify which custom code is actively used and which business processes call specific ABAP objects. On average, 40-60% of custom code remains unused in productive landscapes, making SCMON essential for cleanup activities before migration projects like SAP S/4HANA transformations.

Key Capabilities

  • Tracks usage frequency of ABAP objects
  • Identifies calling business transactions and processes
  • Provides data for custom code decommissioning decisions
  • Supports long-term usage analysis when combined with transaction SUSG

 Configuration Steps (Local Activation (Direct in ABAP System)

  • Execute transaction SCMON or /SDF/SCMON (for NW < 7.4 SP13)
  • Click the Activate button

  • Configure activation settings:
  • Scheduled Deactivation: Specify when monitoring will automatically stop
  • Record Limit: Set maximum number of records (default: 200 million)
  • Save the configuration

Configure Time Slices

  • In transaction SCMON, navigate to ABAP Call Monitor > Configure Time Slices

  • Set Maximum Number of days for data retention (default: 7 days, recommended: up to 365 for detailed analysis)

  • Time slices enable analysis of monitoring progress over specific intervals
  • SCMON automatically deletes data older than the configured retention period

Note - Please complete the required configuration steps in both corresponding systems so that the comparison for the usual analysis can be performed.

Extraction of Call Monitor Data Report –

  • Click on button Display data

 

  • Provide the required date range and other filter if needed.

 

 

  • Download the result set into Excel format

 Analysis of Objects Utilized in the Extract

  • Extract the result from both comparison system.
  • Identify unique objects from both extract result.
  • Finally perform comparison it identifies which objects were not executed/tested

SUMMARY

 

Sum of CountColumn Labels  
Row LabelsTesting does not seem to performedTesting ExecutedGrand Total
Batch Job351954
CLAS23335
FUGR52530
PROG42140182
Remote Function Call9211
Submit Report2294116
Transaction50157207
URL112
Grand Total166471637

Note: - All the above activities are related to Excel operations. There are multiple possible approaches to perform this analysis no strict rule as such.

entry.title: Re: Custom Workflow for Purchase Requisition Based on Hea...
entry.content:
Hello @Shujaat_Haider ,

please find below three BADIs that can be used to create custom workflows in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF

Providing additional conditions for scenarios

SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL

Value evaluation of additional conditions for scenarios

MMPUR_WORKFLOW_AGENTS_V2

Determination of Workflow Agents for Purchasing Documents. Might be necessary if you also want to include WF Receivers that are not available in standard.

 

I hope this helps.

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: Accumulated Static Actuals in SAC Planning using Data...
entry.content:
@shivam8787 

From a performance point of view, Do we really need #PrevLTD and #Actuals as the script is already calculating Lifetodate for each period and will be available for next period calculation and current month Actuals are already available.

Will the below not work ?

FOREACH [d/Date]

DATA([d/Measures] = "Life_To_Date") =
        RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Life_To_Date",[d/Date]=PREVIOUS(1)) +
        RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Actual")

ENDFOR

 Regards

Nikhil

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAP Inbound web service on the client certificate
entry.content:
The below information involves

  • private key/certificate generation
  • certificate/user mapping in S4
  • certificate maintain in S4
  • SOAMANAGER configuration in S4

Normally the first three tasks are done by SAP Basis and the last step is taking care by SAP Integration developers. Please make sure you have relevant skillset and access in the system before go through the document.

Prerequisit

install openssl. This is the tool used to generated private key and self signed certificate.,

1. Switch on ‘Certificate Mapping Rule’ in S4

TCODE: RZ11

Parameter: login/certificate_mapping_rulebased

Make sure the value is 1. If it is default value 0, change it to 1.

2. Create a system user

TCODE: SU01

Make sure the user has enough roles

3. Generate Relevant Self Signed Certificate and Private Key

Of course, it doesn't have to be a self signed certificate. You can have the certificate signed by a Certificate Authority(CA) for the certificate of your firm. Normally you need to provide information of your firm to the CA and the sign process is not free of charge.

For our case, we use a free self signed certificate and no need to involve any CAs.

Step 1. Create a private key

openssl genrsa -out private.key 2048

Step 2. Generate self signed certificate

openssl req -new -x509 -key private.key -out client.crt -days 365

here is an example. The custom value has been highlighted.

  • Country Name (2 letter code) [AU]:AU
  • State or Province Name (full name) [Some-State]:Victoria
  • Locality Name (eg, city) []:Melbourne
  • Organization Name (eg, company) [Internet Widgits Pty Ltd]:Google
  • Organizational Unit Name (eg, section) []:IT
  • Common Name (e.g. server FQDN or YOUR name) []:TEST01
  • Email Address []:TEST01@GOOGLE.COM

This is a screen shot of OpenSSL

Just make CN value as the user id created above

now we have two files:

  • private.key
  • client.crt

4. Configure Certificate and User Mapping

TCODE: CERTRULE

Click change button, then click the button to import the client certificate. 

Click rule button

map CN with username

 

This is the result after saving

5. Import client certificate to STRUST

in order to be able to generate a successful connection, the S4 server certificate should be imported to the client system normally. Otherwise, the client system might not trust S4 endpoint exposed. This process is nothing to do with the steps above.

TCODE: STRUST

Click the change button. Then click view ‘SSL server Standard’. Then click button Import Certificate

 

this is the certificate uploaded

click save button

6. Unit Test

6.1 Expose a SOAP service

Let's take BusinessPartnerERPByIDQueryResponse_In for test.

TCODE: SOAMANAGER

just enable X.509 Client Certificate

get the access URL

 

 

 

6.2 Setup POSTMAN

Get hostname.

TCODE: SMICM. Get the hostname:port

then import the private key and certificate into POSTMAN

For testing,  you should form a request message similar like this:

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
    <soap:Body>
        <n0:BusinessPartnerERPByIDQuery_sync xmlns:n0="http://sap.com/xi/APPL/Global2">
            <MessageHeader>
                <CreationDateTime/>
            </MessageHeader>
            <BusinessPartnerSelectionByID>
                <InternalID>IC0003</InternalID>
            </BusinessPartnerSelectionByID>
        </n0:BusinessPartnerERPByIDQuery_sync>
    </soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>

send the requst without authentication and you will get the response the S4 server.

 

Conclusion.

SAP SOAP based inbound web service supports client certificate.

entry.title: Re: NSE und DataAging Felder
entry.content:
If tables contain frequently accessed (hot) areas and other areas with less frequent accessed (warm) these ranges can be separated by partitioning.
The possibility to satisfy a query with accessing only a subset of partitions is also called partition pruning.

Refer more details about the "Static and Dynamic Partition Pruning"
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/602e0dcb40364401a092329296405b84.html 

Designing Partitions: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/66a1819e7eda4151ace6c02a72887fcd.html 

Also refer the below related KBAs :

2044468 - FAQ: SAP HANA Partitioning
2799997 - FAQ: SAP HANA Native Storage Extension
2973243 - Guidance for use of SAP HANA Native Storage Extension in SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite powered by SAP HANA

Hope this is helpful.

entry.title: Meet Your HANA Cloud Lifecycle Agent: Enabled by Joule and SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content:
In a previous blog post, we explored how SAP HANA Cloud lifecycle management can be automated end-to-end using SAP Automation Pilot, outlining various automations capabilities and scenarios:  from instance operations to backup and recovery. We also have demonstrated how SAP Automation Pilot can help Ops / DevOps / IT Admin team to bring Agentic Ops into action, see this blogpost: From Automation to Agentic Ops: MCP Servers in SAP Automation Pilot

Now, it is time to take the next step. What happens when all available HANA Cloud automations in SAP Automation Pilot are no longer triggered manually, but instead are consumed and orchestrated by AI agents? 

Welcome to the world of HANA Cloud Ops Agents powered by Joule and SAP Automation Pilot and a true HANA Cloud Lifecycle Management agent - powered by SAP Automation Pilot content and consumed directly in Joule in a truly agentic manner.

🎥  Meet Your HANA Cloud Lifecycle Management Agent

See an example about HANA Cloud Lifecycle Agent enabled by SAP Automation Pilot and find out how easy it is to set up an MCP server, expose automations, and trigger them in an agentic way through Joule.

 

From Automation to Agentic HANA Cloud Operations

With SAP Automation Pilot, DevOps and Ops teams can already automate complex operational workflows using prebuilt and custom commands. At the same time, Joule is evolving into an intelligent orchestrator of AI agents that can observe, reason, and act across SAP landscapes. Bringing these two together unlocks a new operational model where Joule Agents orchestrate and trigger automations based on intent and context (see diagram below): 

SAP HANA Cloud operations typically involve a wide range of recurring, critical activities. With Automation Pilot and Joule Agents, these can now be exposed as reusable, governed capabilities.

Let’s look at some concrete examples: 

nstead of executing repetitive and sensitive database operations manually, you can now automate domains such as: 🔹 Instance Lifecycle Management 🔹Backup & Recovery 🔹 Snapshot Management 🔹 Instance Configuration🔹 Upgrades & Plugins 🔹 Elastic Compute Nodes (ECN), etc. 

…and Many More

These scenarios are just a starting point.

Any operational workflow automated in SAP Automation Pilot can be exposed via MCP Servers and consumed by Joule Agents - enabling a flexible, extensible, and truly agent-driven operations model across SAP HANA Cloud and beyond.

This approach unlocks a completely new way to ops  automation  where AI agents can reason, execute, and orchestrate operational tasks seamlessly following customers requests and needs.

Learn More

If you would like to explore MCP servers in more detail or start using them in your own landscapes, please check out the SAP Automation Pilot official documentation which provides further insights:

  • MCP Server Overview;
  • Managing MCP Servers;
  • Integrating SAP Automation Pilot with Joule Studio ;

Looking forward to collaborating and shaping the future of agentic IT operations together.

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud Sourcing & Procurement: How to Stay Notified
entry.content:
As a Sourcing & Procurement expert - whether you are a key user, consultant, or application manager - it is essential to stay informed about the latest developments, corrections, and known issues in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. 

SAP provides a convenient way to stay up to date via notifications for SAP Notes and Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs) using SAP for Me. KBA Note 2771695 explains how to set up these notifications.

In this blog post, I would like to highlight relevant components for Sourcing & Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that you may want to include in your custom notification view. 


Relevant Components for Sourcing & Procurement:

MM-FIO-PUR-PO-2CL

Fiori UI for Purchase Orders (Public Cloud)

MM-FIO-PUR-REL-2CL

Fiori UI for Purchasing Approval (Public Cloud)

MM-FIO-PUR-SQ-2CL

Fiori UI for Sources of Supply (Public Cloud)

MM-FIO-PUR-RFQ-2CL

Fiori UI for RFQ/Quotation (Public Cloud)

MM-FIO-PUR-SQ-CON-CL

Fiori UI for Manage Purchase Contracts (Public Cloud)

MM-PUR-PO-2CL

Purchase Orders (Public Cloud)

MM-PUR-PO-WFL-2CL

Purchase Order Workflow (Public Cloud)

MM-PUR-REQ-2CL

Purchase Requisitions (Public Cloud)

MM-PUR-REQ-PRF-2CL

Manage Purchase Requisitions – Professional (Public Cloud)

MM-PUR-REQ-SOC-2CL

Fiori UI for Sourcing Purchase Requisitions (Public Cloud)

MM-PUR-GF-SCR-2CL

Subcontracting in Purchasing (Repair) (Public Cloud)

MM-PUR-GF-ACC-2CL

Account Assignment (Public Cloud)

LE-SHP-DL-2CL

Delivery Processing (Public Cloud)

 

Set Up Guide for a custom view with screenshots:

 

 

 

Summary: 

Staying informed about SAP Notes and KBAs is critical for smooth operations in S/4HANA Public Cloud Sourcing & Procurement. By subscribing to relevant components in SAP for Me, you can ensure that important updates are not missed.  
 This list is a good starting point, however it is not exhaustive and can be extended depending on your specific scope and responsibilities. 

Links:

  • 2771695 - How to get notifications about new SAP Notes and KBAs - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: How to enable an automatic email of an Invoice, IRN a...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Our internal chatbot has provided the following response. Please check whether it helps you resolve the issue:

To achieve the automatic emailing of invoice and IRN/E-waybill PDFs to multiple recipients, follow these steps:

Step 1: Document Generation

  • Customer Invoice (PDF): Ensure that the system is configured to generate a PDF of the customer invoice upon billing.
  • IRN Document: Capture the Invoice Reference Number (IRN) as part of the e-invoicing process.
  • E-Way Bill Document (PDF): Generate the E-Way Bill document as a PDF. 

Step 2: Automatic Email Trigger

  • Configure the system to automatically trigger an email once the billing document is successfully created. This can be done using the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Output Management framework.

Step 3: Multiple Recipients Handling

  • Custom Logic Implementation: Use the Business Add-In (BAdI) Addition of Email Recipients to implement custom logic that sends the billing document output to multiple email addresses, including CC and BCC recipients.
  • Output Parameter Determination: In the Output Parameter Determination app, set up rules for email recipients. You can specify multiple recipients such as Dispatch In-charge, Area Sales Manager (ASM), and Stores Department.

Example Code for Custom Logic

DATA: additional_email_recipient LIKE LINE OF additionalemailrecipients.

" Set email recipient dynamically
additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailrole = 'TO'.
additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailaddress = 'dispatch_incharge@example.com'.
APPEND additional_email_recipient TO additionalemailrecipients.

additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailrole = 'CC'.
additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailaddress = 'asm@example.com'.
APPEND additional_email_recipient TO additionalemailrecipients.

additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailrole = 'BCC'.
additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailaddress = 'stores@example.com'.
APPEND additional_email_recipient TO additionalemailrecipients.

Final Steps

  • Activate Changes: Ensure that all changes are activated in the Output Parameter Determination app.
  • Test the Configuration: Perform tests to ensure that the emails are sent automatically to the specified recipients upon billing document creation.

By following these steps, you can automate the process of sending invoices and related documents to multiple recipients efficiently.

For more information, see the following sources:

India - Invoice Reference Number Capturing
India - Creating Electronic Way Bill (eWay Bill)
Sales - How to Add Additional Email Recipients for Billing Documents by Using Custom Logic

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: More Power, New Name: The UI Flexibility Service Explained
entry.content:
SAPUI5 flexibility for adapting standard SAP apps

SAPUI5 is SAP’s strategic Web framework for SAP Fiori. SAPUI5 flexibility allows developers, key users, and end users to adapt standard apps with simple drag-and-drop tools. Discover all the capabilities here: SAPUI5 Flexibility - All You Need to Know.

 

Key user adaptation

Key user adaptation is built into SAP Fiori Launchpad / SAP Build Work Zone and is included commercially as part of the respective product. Outside these products, the same capabilities are available as a dedicated service, UI5 flexibility for key users, on SAP BTP, enabling key user adaptation for standalone apps, for example, for SAP partners building their own apps. The service is available via BTPEA, BTP Trial, and partner TD&D licenses. See details in the SAP Discovery Center.

 

What's new?

Some time ago, we added the end user personalization capability to the UI5 flexibility for key users service. This allows also business users to easily change the UI of standard apps, helping them work more efficiently. This created confusion, as the service name UI5 flexibility for key users no longer reflects the full range of benefits. We therefore decided to rename the service:

"UI5 flexibility for key users" ➔ "UI flexibility service"

You will see the name being changed now across all SAP assets, e.g.:

 

Apart from the name, nothing changes in the scope or functionality of the UI flexibility service.

entry.title: Re: RAP Translation Issue – XLF Maintained but Labels Not...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

Our internal chatbot has provided the following response. Can you please check whether it resolves your issue?

It seems you are facing issues with translations not reflecting in the UI for a managed RAP application in S/4HANA Public Cloud. Here are some steps and considerations that might help resolve the issue:

Steps to Ensure Translations Reflect

  1. Check Metadata Extension: Ensure that the metadata extension is correctly linked to your CDS views and that the translations are properly defined using @EndUserText.label.

  2. Verify XLF File: Double-check the XLF file for any errors or missing translations. Ensure that the file is correctly formatted and includes all necessary translations.

  3. Publish Translations: After uploading the XLF file, make sure to publish the translations using the Maintain Translations app. This step is crucial for the translations to be active.

  4. Clear Cache: Sometimes, the issue might be related to caching. Try clearing the browser cache or using a different browser to see if the translations appear.

  5. Check UI Annotations: Ensure that the fields exposed via UI annotations are correctly configured to use the translated labels.

  6. Logon Language: Confirm that the logon language is set to the target language where translations are expected to reflect.

Additional Considerations

  • Limitations: There might be specific limitations in RAP applications regarding translations. Ensure that all annotations and configurations are compatible with the RAP framework.

  • SAP Translation Hub: Consider using SAP Translation Hub for more comprehensive translation management.

For more information, see Maintain Translations.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Tax Debit Journal Billing SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
From the SD side, only one condition type is passed to FI during billing. The FI posting then interprets that condition according to the account determination rules. If you want an additional tax line (like VAT‑out Wapu Account) to appear in the debit column, this cannot be achieved “automatically” without configuration.
There is no standard switch in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to add an extra debit tax line without customizing. The usual way is to create another tax condition type in SD pricing procedure and assign the correct account key so that FI generates the extra posting line.

So the answer is:
Standard system behavior is one condition type → one FI posting line. If you need an extra debit line, you must configure a new condition type in the pricing procedure with its own account key. There is no standard option to add it without configuration.

entry.title: Re: Use APP Create Payment Requisitions to Query open pay...
entry.content:
Hello @jesseho271 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, Create Payment Requisition app supports querying open payables with payment amount information.

If your goal is to provide the result of that query as an interface for an external system, the quickest way to identify the CDS source is to use the View Browser app.

In View Browser, search with business terms like supplier, open payable, payment amount, due date, etc. Review the candidate CDS views. Open the CDS details and check the fields, associations, underlying artifacts and its release status.

You can also check Query Browser if you are trying to get a result based on analytical query rather than only a transactional UI source. You can search for relevant payables or payment related queries and then inspect the sources you require.

If you need external interface, ensure you use only released CDS content.

You can then expose it with the External API scenario. Do not try to expose the internal app data provider directly, instead, rebuild the required output using released CDS sources in Custom CDS Views and publish that as your external API.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/7139c1648de84c0bbd7637589ed7c92a.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/e30de6eae4d24d70b65996ac8ff88848.html?locale=en-US

So, to summarise your use case, the fastest path is :

  • Make use of View Browser to find the CDS and associations
  • Use Query Browser if the source is analytical
  • Custom CDS Views plus External API to deliver the interface you seek in a supported way.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing enablement: Where to start, what to use, and how to learn effectively
entry.content:
When getting started with SAP Digital Manufacturing, one of the first questions is often: “Where do I actually begin?”

Between learning platforms, webinars, documentation, and community content, the amount of available enablement material can feel overwhelming. The challenge is usually not a lack of resources, but knowing what is relevant, where to start, and how everything fits together.

This blog brings together a set of key enablement resources for SAP Digital Manufacturing and provides a simple way to navigate them, whether you are just getting started or looking to deepen your knowledge.

 

Getting started: First steps and onboarding resources

If you are just getting started with SAP Digital Manufacturing, there are a few resources that can help you move from orientation to actual system access and setup much faster.

The Onboarding Resource Center provides a structured overview of key onboarding materials, guidance, and entry points. For a more hands-on, step-by-step approach, the Provisioning Guide is particularly useful. 

This guide walks you through:

  • how to access and provision your system
  • key assets and required setup steps
  • relevant contacts and communities that can support you along the way

 

Get a first overview and feel

When getting started with SAP Digital Manufacturing, it can be helpful to begin with a guided introduction.

A good starting point is to look at SAP Learning platform for  structured, role-based learning journeys and courses. 

Start with the learning journey “Applying SAP Digital Manufacturing” which helps to:

  • understand the overall positioning and key capabilities of SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • get an overview of core functionalities, including insights and data collection
  • learn how to configure and analyse master data and shop floor processes
  • explore integration, extensibility, and machine connectivity

This gives you a solid foundation across both business and technical aspects of SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Parallelly explore:

  • SAP Help Portal / documentation for detailed guidance during implementation
  • FAQ on SAP Digital Manufacturing to review frequently asked questions across administrative, integration, technical, functional, and industry topics.

📍At this stage, the goal is: “I understand how the product works and what I can do with it.”

 

Go deeper: Explore and stay up to date (without overwhelm)

SAP solutions evolve continuously, which means learning doesn’t stop after one round. After building a foundation, most questions become more practical:

  • How does this work in a real scenario?
  • What are best practices?
  • What should I watch out for?

This is where you move into deeper formats:

  • Join webinars and expert sessions for real-world examples and walkthroughs → Visit our SCM Engagement Hub to explore release-based What’s New sessions as well as Meet the Expert webcasts providing deep-dives into certain topics.  For instance, for the upcoming 2605 release we have planned two What's New webinars on POD 2.0 as well as EBR and eDHR. Register here. 
  • Keep an eye on SAP Community blog posts on SAP Digital Manufacturing → to get context, learn more about specific use cases, and get the expert perspectives

❗For more frequent, practical insights from everyday work in SAP Digital Manufacturing, it’s also worth following voices from the community that post frequently. For example, my colleague Manuel Costa regularly shares hands-on perspectives and useful learnings. Don’t miss out on his regular content!

📍This stage is about: “How do I actually apply what I’ve learned?” and “What do I need to know now to stay effective in my role?”

 

A simple way to combine everything

If you’re unsure how to bring all of this together on a regular basis, here is a simple approach that works well in practice:

  1. Start with the What’s New viewer to learn about the latest innovations
  2. Register for the upcoming What’s New webcasts to learn about what’s coming in the next release
  3. Use SAP Digital Manufacturing topic page as an entry point for L2 & L3 decks updated on release basis, roadmap explorer, industry spotlight as well as selected legacy materials.
  4. Register and (re-)watch our Meet the Expert webcasts to see real-world applications
  5. Refer to documentation when implementing
  6. Join SAP Community and follow key contributors to stay up to date

Combining this resources this way helps create a clear and efficient learning flow.

 

Final thoughts

SAP Digital Manufacturing offers a wide range of enablement resources, the key is not to use everything at once, but to focus on what is most relevant for your role and current needs.

💡We continuously evolve our enablement offerings based on feedback from customers and partners.
If you have questions, are looking for specific resources, or would like to suggest topics for future webcasts, feel free to reach out to us at: engage-manufacturing@sap.com.  We’d be happy to hear your thoughts and continuously improve our content based on your input.

entry.title: Re: Multiple outbound delivery for one STO
entry.content:
Good day @aravindabv 

Thank you for your question.

The Public Cloud generally does not provide the possibility for you to create a single outbound delivery for STO line items that have different delivery dates.

The reason for that restriction is because stock transport processing is driven by schedule line level delivery dates, and outbound deliveries are created with reference to the STO based on those due dates. If STO are due on different dates, the system normally creates separate OBDs rather than grouping them into one. This reflects delivery and transportation scheduling method in Public Cloud.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/23ff99da6daf4fc4ae2ac251eb9b15c2.html?locale=en-US

So, basically what that means is:

  • Same vendor alone is not enough to force one OBD
  • Different delivery dates will usually lead to separate outbound deliveries.

Instead, the alternatives I recommend is:

  1. If one OBD is mandatory, align the delivery dates on the STO items so the lines become due together.
  2. If the different dates are critical to your client’s business, keep separate OBDs and consider consolidation later in transportation execution or freight planning, if that is your actual business need. The Public Cloud provides transportation consolidation capabilities, but that is different from forcing one OBD when you create delivery.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/1203a79ab14d427b9362041ff0f4f33e.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud Public: availability check before posti...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Here's the response our internal chatbot provided. Please check whether it helps you resolve your issue:

To achieve your goal of running a ‘live’ availability check for components before sending production order confirmations, you can consider the following approaches:

  1. API for Availability Check: Currently, there is no specific released API in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that directly performs an availability check for components similar to the check done during production order confirmation/backflush. However, you can use the available APIs to gather stock information and perform checks programmatically.

  2. Using API_MATERIAL_STOCK_SRV: This API can be used to retrieve stock snapshots by material, plant, storage location, and batch. While it does not fully replicate the confirmation logic, it can provide the necessary stock levels to determine availability. You can use this data to calculate shortages and display messages in your MES.

  3. Custom CDS View for Failed Goods Movements: You can build a custom CDS view on top of the I_FailedGoodsMovementItem view to expose it as a custom OData API. This approach is supported in the Public Cloud. The high-level steps include:

    • Create a Custom CDS View.
    • Publish it as an API.
    • Set up a Communication Scenario and Arrangement.

  4. Retrieving Error Messages: For retrieving message long texts for failed items, you can explore associations and fields available in the CDS view or use standard SAP functionalities to fetch error details.

Best Practices

  • Integration with MES: Ensure that your MES system can handle the logic for checking component availability using the stock data retrieved from SAP.
  • Error Handling: Implement robust error handling in your MES to manage failed confirmations and provide clear feedback to users.

For more detailed guidance, you may need to consult SAP documentation or engage with SAP support for specific implementation advice. 

For more information, see the following sources:

Production Planning and Control - How to Check the Component Availability
Production Planning and Control - Availability Check

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: How to Upgrade SAP BW Knowledge to SAP BW/4HANA and Get Certified
entry.content:
How to Upgrade SAP BW Knowledge to SAP BW/4HANA and Get Certified

SAP BW is a powerful enterprise data warehousing solution designed to consolidate, transform, and manage data from diverse sources. This data is then used for reporting, analysis, and informed decision-making.

SAP BW/4HANA represents a complete redesign, built exclusively on SAP HANA. This version prioritizes simplification and streamlining, reducing the complexity of data modeling and management. The number of data objects and layers is significantly reduced, making the system easier to understand and maintain.

So how can you upgrade your SAP BW knowledge to SAP BW/4HANA?

The SAP Certified - SAP BW/4HANA Delta certification verifies that you possess the knowledge of implementing and modeling SAP BW/4HANA required by the profile of an SAP BW/4HANA consultant.

Online Self Study

For online self study, you can use the learning journey Exploring Migration Paths from SAP BW to SAP BW/4HANA to prepare for the certification.

In this learning journey, you build your knowledge step-by-step with online courses, containing explanations using texts, videos and exercise simulations.

The following online courses are needed to prepare for the certification:

  • Getting Started Building an On-Premise Data Warehouse using SAP BW/4HANA
  • Upgrading Your SAP BW Skills to SAP BW/4HANA

Instructor-led Classroom

As an alternative, you can attend an instructor-led classroom training (on-site or virtual), where you can also work through lots of exercises on a provided SAP system.

The following instructor-led training is needed to prepare for the certification:

  • DBW462: Data Warehousing with SAP BW/4HANA - Delta from SAP BW 7.5 classic to SAP BW/4HANA

Hands-on Practice

If you want to practice outside the setting of a classroom training, SAP Practice Systems offer to practice using the similar exercises that are used in the instructor-led class.

Just click below to explore practice systems:

https://learning.sap.com/practice-systems?page=1

Live Sessions

In addition to courses and practice systems, the SAP Learning portal offers live sessions that are useful for learning guidelines, ask questions and deepen your knowledge on specific topics. These sessions are delivered by subject-matter-experts (SMEs), either from SAP Training, SAP Partners or other experts at SAP such as Product Managers.

The sessions come under five categories:

  • Get Started
  • Expert Deep Dive
  • What's New
  • Get Certified
  • Stay Certified

Just click below to explore the live sessions:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions?page=1

Certification Exam

When you feel prepared enough, it's time to take the certification exam.

SAP has now moved this certification towards a practice-based exam which replaces the traditional theoretical/knowledge-based exams (multi-choice questions).

To get certified, you need to complete activities in an SAP BW/4HANA system.

It's still important to get started with studying the learning journey to understand SAP BW/4HANA. As addition, use the SAP Practice Systems to prepare for the practice-based exam.

Just click below for info about the SAP Certified - SAP BW/4HANA Delta certification:

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-specialist-sap-bw-4hana-delta

Community

We are looking forward to getting your feedback on these resources aimed at getting your level higher in the area of SAP BW/4HANA. Please feel free to use this new community and connect with us and other learners. Also let us know about topics you would like to have covered in future live sessions, so that we can make our content even more suited to your needs.

Happy learning with SAP!

entry.title: Need to Know - Unlocking the Power of AI in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
A practical guide to the AI capabilities already at your fingertips inside SAP Analytics Cloud (aka SAP Business Data Cloud)

Artificial Intelligence is no longer something you need to bolt onto your analytics platform — in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), it’s already embedded directly into the product. These AI capabilities are designed to help business users work faster, gain deeper insights, and make better decisions, without needing data science skills or complex setup.

The information below is a summary of the new exciting AI features inside SAP Analytics Cloud for both reporting (Business Intelligence) and planning use cases.  Clearly SAP will add more AI features , which I will add and update this blog once they appear.  At the end of this blog there is more information about how to use these features in your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.

 

Ask questions in plain language with Just Ask

One of the biggest barriers to analytics adoption is technical complexity. Just Ask, SAP Analytics Cloud’s natural language query capability, removes that barrier.

Instead of building queries or knowing where data lives, users can simply ask questions in natural language, such as:

  • “Sales by region this quarter”
  • “Compare actuals versus forecast last year”
  • “Revenue for Germany and France by month”

SAP Analytics Cloud automatically returns answers as clear charts and tables, which can then be:

  • Added to stories
  • Exported to Excel or CSV
  • Explored further in the Data Analyzer

Why it matters:
Just Ask makes analytics accessible to everyone — not just power users — and dramatically reduces the time it takes to get answers from data.

 

Plan smarter with Predictive Planning

Planning is all about the future — and AI plays a major role here. Predictive Planning in SAP Analytics Cloud uses automated machine learning to turn historical data into accurate forecasts.

You can:

  • Train predictive models directly on your planning data
  • Generate forecasts at the right business level (for example, per product or entity)
  • Use predictions as a baseline and adjust them based on business knowledge

Why it matters:
Predictive Planning reduces bias, improves forecast accuracy, and helps planning teams react faster to change — all without manual calculation effort.

 

Understand why numbers change with Smart Insights

Charts show what happened — but often not why. Smart Insights fills that gap.

With a single click on a data point or variance, Smart Insights automatically analyzes the underlying data and produces:

  • A written explanation in plain language
  • Supporting visual breakdowns of contributing factors

These insights appear directly inside your story, exactly where you’re analyzing the data.

Why it matters:
Instead of manually drilling down and interpreting data, users get instant explanations — saving time and improving confidence in decisions.

 

Predict outcomes using Smart Predict

For more advanced predictive use cases, Smart Predict allows users to build and compare predictive models without being data scientists.

It supports three types of predictive scenarios:

  • Classification – predicting whether something will happen (for example, likelihood of churn)
  • Regression – predicting a numeric value (such as revenue or cost)
  • Time Series – forecasting future values over time

You can compare multiple models, choose the best-performing one, and apply it to new data — all through an intuitive interface.

Why it matters:
Smart Predict brings enterprise-grade prediction into the hands of business users, removing the need for external tools or complex modeling.

 

Create forecasts directly inside charts

Sometimes you just want a fast forecast without building a full predictive model. SAP Analytics Cloud makes this easy by allowing users to run forecasts directly in time series or line charts.

Available options include:

  • Automatic forecasting
  • Exponential smoothing (for seasonal patterns)
  • Linear regression

Advanced settings allow you to control forecast periods and even include additional influencing factors.

Why it matters:
This enables quick, visual forecasting during analysis — perfect for ad hoc insights and fast decision-making.

 

 

Discover hidden patterns with Smart Grouping

When working with scatterplots or bubble charts, spotting patterns can be difficult. Smart Grouping solves this by automatically clustering similar data points using AI‑based grouping.

With Smart Grouping, you can:

  • Identify natural clusters in your data
  • Reveal relationships that are hard to see manually
  • Accelerate exploratory analysis

Why it matters:
Users can uncover insights faster and more accurately, especially when dealing with complex or high‑volume datasets.

 

 

Build calculations using AI‑assisted calculations

Writing calculations can be time‑consuming — and understanding existing formulas can be just as challenging. AI‑assisted calculations simplifies both.

Users can:

  • Generate calculation formulas using natural language (“Create a margin percentage measure”)
  • Ask AI to explain existing formulas in plain English

This works directly in the calculation dialog in the Data Analyzer.

Why it matters:
Business users save time, reduce errors, and gain confidence when working with calculations — without needing deep technical knowledge.

 

Automatically summarise charts for PowerPoint

Creating presentations often involves manually summarising charts — a repetitive and time‑consuming task. With the AI‑assisted chart summary in the SAP Analytics Cloud PowerPoint add‑in, this step is automated.

The feature:

  • Generates a concise three‑bullet summary of a chart
  • Inserts it directly into PowerPoint as editable text
  • Can be regenerated whenever the data changes

Why it matters:
Users spend less time writing slide commentary and more time focusing on insights and storytelling.

 

Make comments easier to consume with AI‑assisted commenting

Comments are valuable — but large volumes of them can be hard to process. AI‑assisted commenting helps by:

  • Summarizing multiple comments
  • Aggregating comments across hierarchies
  • Translating comments into the user’s preferred language
  • Rephrasing comments to improve clarity and tone

Why it matters:
This makes collaboration more effective and ensures decision‑makers can quickly understand qualitative context alongside the numbers.

 

Speed up planning logic with AI‑assisted data actions

For planning professionals, AI‑assisted data actions and documentation reduce the effort involved in scripting and documentation.

With AI, you can:

  • Generate Advanced Formula scripts from plain‑language comments
  • Automatically create readable explanations of existing scripts
  • Generate documentation for entire data actions

Why it matters:
This improves productivity, simplifies maintenance, and supports better knowledge transfer across teams.

Simulate and model uncertainty quickly and easily using Compass

Whilst not strictly an AI feature Compass is a great feature that uses Monte Carlo simulation to to estimate the possible outcomes of uncertain business events.   Compass is an embedded feature that uses Monte Carlo simulation to model uncertainty in business drivers and show the probable range of outcomes for key KPIs, supporting risk-aware planning and scenario analysis.

With Compass :

  • Risk‑aware decision making: Runs many simulations quickly and easily to quantify the likelihood of outcomes, not just single best/worst cases. 
  • Easy scenario modeling: Enables no‑code “what‑if” simulations directly in SAC using existing driver–target relationships.   Just right click on a driver in a table, select Compass and you can start simulating the model using your drivers immediately.  It's as quick and easy as that! 
  • Improved planning confidence: Helps planners assess the impact of volatile drivers (e.g., costs, demand, inflation) across budgeting, target setting, and strategy
     

Why it matters:

Compass moves planning beyond static assumptions to a probabilistic view of the future, allowing organizations to make more confident, resilient decisions in the face of uncertainty.
 
Help about Compass can be found here https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/18850a0e13944f53aa8a8b7c094ea29e/3cc76bccb94648e4a3952029a7e9425c.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=LATEST 
 

 

 

More Information

Discover More - To learn more about each specific AI feature then go to the SAP Discovery Centre here https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog/?productId=f754af8c-a3a2-4bf7-bbd7-2face9ca4139 (the link is filtered on SAP Analytics Cloud Business AI features except Compass).  This excellent site provides information about the SAP Business AI features for all of SAP’s solutions, not just SAP Analytics Cloud.  

The site provides an overview of the specific feature including links to the help portal, business value overview of the feature, roadmap information, customer stories and more.  You can also find out whether the feature is already included in the SAP Analytics Cloud license or where you need to pay an additional cost (called AI units) to use the feature:

  • Base – means that AI Units are not currently required to use this AI offering.
  • Premium – means that AI Units are required to be purchased to use this AI offering.

Enable AI-Assisted Features - AI-assisted features are integrated to generate AI content in various areas of SAP Analytics Cloud. For example, you might want to obtain a summary of all comments in a lengthy comment thread. To enable your users to access these features, you need to go through an activation procedure. Go here to see how you enable these AI features https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/18850a0e13944f53aa8a8b7c094ea29e/8dcc1f1915b241b3a10c8e5b8a76b062.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST 

AI-Assisted Features Help - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/18850a0e13944f53aa8a8b7c094ea29e/00ca4ae380794468ba42ea46d10a4045.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST 

SAP Community - In addition to the help portal we have an excellent community site for additional help, blogs, tricks and tips for using these features - Artificial Intelligence for Planning and Analytics https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/cloud-analytics/ai-for-planning-and-analytics 

 

Tips about using AI Features

As you embark on your AI adventure with SAP Analytics Cloud here are some useful tips to help you and your users adopt them...

  • AI is the tool, not the mission.  AI should be implemented to enhance human productivity, accelerate workflows, or solve specific problems, rather than adopting AI simply because it is the current trend.
  • Start your AI Journey by Experimenting with how AI can help your users. The features described above is AI embedded into the solution.  It’s quick and easy to activate and easy to use, meaning that you can try it out and experiment with your users without a lot of setup required.         
  • Execute over ambition.  Because this AI is quick and easy to activate and experiment with it means that you can execute on using AI with real use cases straight away.
  • Done is better than perfect.  Start and experiment "Perfect is the enemy of good"  meaning that striving for flawlessness can prevent the completion of worthwhile, functional tasks. Perfectionism leads to procrastination, inefficiency, and missed opportunities, whereas aiming for "good enough" allows for progress, implementation, and future improvements.
  • Don't boil the ocean.  Focus on a few things and execute well on these.
  • People over Technology.  You need people to adapt and adopt to the AI.  Otherwise it's useless and a waste of money.  Education and experimentation is the key to successful adoption

entry.title: Re: Multiple outbound delivery for one STO
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the system behavior you’re seeing is standard and expected: when multiple line items for the same material have different requested delivery dates, the system generates separate Outbound Deliveries (OBDs). This is because delivery creation logic groups items by criteria such as shipping point, ship‑to party, and delivery date.

Also check SAP notes 3310234 and 3551006
